task_completion.description: Configure AndroidManifest.xml to accept shared text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The AndroidManifest.xml file is open in an editor, showing an `<intent-filter>` inside the MainActivity block. It must contain `<action android:name="android.intent.action.SEND" />` and `<data android:mimeType="text/plain" />`.

task_completion.description: Check for the SEND action and text/plain type in MainActivity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: MainActivity.kt is open in an editor, showing code that verifies the incoming intent (e.g., `if (intent?.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND && intent.type == "text/plain")`).

task_completion.description: Extract the shared text from the intent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: MainActivity.kt shows code extracting the text from the intent, typically using `intent.getStringExtra(Intent.EXTRA_TEXT)`.

task_completion.description: Display the extracted text in the noteInput EditText.
task_completion.visual_evidence: MainActivity.kt shows code assigning the extracted text to the EditText, such as `findViewById<EditText>(R.id.noteInput).setText(...)` or using a binding object.
A 'BUILD SUCCESSFUL' message or a green checkmark indicating a successful Gradle build is visible in the Android Studio Build tool window or terminal.

integrity.description: The text is dynamically extracted from the intent, not hardcoded.
